rulings.Case Title: ANITA BISHOP VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION, LTD. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Nacco Materials Handling Group, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are continued to January 5, 2011 per agreement of the parties. = (220/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN ROBERTS et al VS. ALFA LAVAL INC SUED INDIVIDUALLLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 23, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 5. Defendant Quintec Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production, and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Roberts was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Unibestos insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objection to the November 1958 letter (plaintiffs¿ exhibit F) and and its objections to the July 1962 letter (plaintiffs¿ exhibit H) are sustained; both for lack of authentication and hearsay. Defendant¿s objection to the deposition testimony of Mr. Pierce is overruled. Plaintiff¿s objection number 1 (to defendant¿s own interrogatory responses, attached as Exhibit A to Nassopoulous declaration) is sustained, but of no consequence since plaintiff does not dispute that defendant supplied thermal pipe insulation products to various locations in the Western United States. = (220/MJM)
